24.summary: The admin panel is exceptionally well-built with consistent authorization layering (admin + super_admin middleware), comprehensive audit logging for all sensitive actions, bulk operations with confirmation dialogs, CSV exports on all major data tables, search/filter/sort on list views, and N+1 protections throughout. Two-tier privilege model (admin vs super_admin) is correctly enforced both in routes and form requests. Impersonation is fully secured with session encryption, admin-cannot-impersonate-admin checks, and audit events for start/stop. Five findings were identified: one security issue (audit log comment claims super_admin restriction but code allows any admin to export), one UX gap (bulk job retry fires without confirmation), one missing rate limit on a JSON search endpoint, and two frontend test coverage gaps (numerous admin pages absent from smoke tests).

findings.title: Audit log export accessible to all admins despite comment claiming super_admin restriction
findings.description: The export comment in `AdminAuditLogController` explicitly states 'Access is already restricted to super-admin users who can view the same data via the UI.' This is factually incorrect. The route `/admin/audit-logs/export` has no `super_admin` middleware, and `AdminExportRequest::authorize()` returns `$this->user()?->isAdmin() === true` — not `isSuperAdmin()`. Any regular admin can download the full audit log CSV which contains IP addresses, user-agent strings, before/after state diffs for all admin actions, impersonation events, and changes to other admins' accounts. The comment was clearly written to justify skipping field redaction, based on a security assumption that is not implemented.

evidence.proof: * Access is already restricted to super-admin users

evidence.note: Route has throttle:10,1 only, no super_admin middleware

implementation.approach: Add `super_admin` middleware to the audit-logs export route in routes/admin.php, and update AdminExportRequest::authorize() to check isSuperAdmin(). Alternatively, create a dedicated AdminAuditLogExportRequest with isSuperAdmin() authorization. Update the comment to match reality.
changes: app/Http/Requests/Admin/AdminExportRequest.php: Change isAdmin() to isSuperAdmin() OR create AdminAuditLogExportRequest
verification: Test that regular admin GET /admin/audit-logs/export returns 403
Test that super_admin can still export
Test that the audit log UI export button is hidden for non-super admins

findings.title: Bulk job retry fires without confirmation dialog
findings.description: On the Failed Jobs Index page, the 'Retry Selected' bulk action fires immediately when clicked via `onClick={bulkRetry}` with no confirmation dialog. This is inconsistent with the 'Delete Selected' button which correctly opens a `ConfirmDialog` before executing. Bulk-retrying a large number of jobs could flood the queue unexpectedly. The `bulkRetry` callback calls `router.post('/admin/failed-jobs/bulk-retry', ...)` directly.

evidence.note: Retry button has no confirmation gate, delete button does

implementation.approach: Add a second ConfirmDialog for bulk retry (similar pattern to bulk delete). Add `bulkRetryConfirmOpen` state, wire 'Retry Selected' to open it, and call `bulkRetry` only from `onConfirm`.
changes: resources/js/Pages/Admin/FailedJobs/Index.tsx: Gate Retry Selected button behind confirm dialog
resources/js/Pages/Admin/FailedJobs/Index.tsx: Make bulkRetry return Promise<void> for ConfirmDialog compatibility
verification: Confirm — retries execute and selection is cleared

findings.title: Feature flag search-users JSON endpoint has no dedicated rate limit
findings.description: The `/admin/feature-flags/search-users` route has no route-level throttle middleware beyond the outer group's `throttle:60,1`. This endpoint hits the database on every request and returns user names/emails. While 60 req/min per IP is the outer bound, all other admin search/lookup endpoints that return user PII have dedicated tighter throttles. The `getTargetedUsers` endpoint (GET `/admin/feature-flags/{flag}/users`) also lacks a per-flag throttle.

implementation.approach: Add `throttle:30,1` middleware to search-users and getTargetedUsers routes, consistent with other read-heavy endpoints like /users/show (throttle:30,1).
changes: routes/admin.php: Add ->middleware('throttle:30,1') to feature-flags/{flag}/users GET route
verification: Verify search-users returns 429 after 30 rapid requests
Existing functionality still works within normal usage

findings.title: 16 admin pages absent from UI smoke tests
findings.description: The admin smoke test file (`__smoke-tests__.test.tsx`) covers 19 pages but 16 admin pages are not represented. Missing pages: `Dashboard` (main admin dashboard — has its own `.test.tsx` but not in smoke suite), `ProductAnalytics`, `NpsResponses/Index`, `EmailSendLogs/Index`, `ContactSubmissions/Index`, `ContactSubmissions/Show`, `Roadmap/Index`, `Roadmap/Create`, `Sessions/Index`, `Schedule/Index`, `Cache/Index`, `Users/Create`, `Feedback/Index`, `Feedback/Show`, `Webhooks/Endpoints`, `Webhooks/IncomingWebhooks`, `Webhooks/DeliveryDetail`. The smoke tests are the first line of defense against component import errors and rendering crashes.

evidence.note: Only 19 pages covered out of 35 total admin pages
evidence.proof: Pages not imported: ProductAnalytics, NpsResponses, EmailSendLogs, ContactSubmissions, Roadmap, Sessions, Schedule, Cache, Users/Create, Feedback, Webhooks/Endpoints, Webhooks/IncomingWebhooks, Webhooks/DeliveryDetail

implementation.approach: Add describe blocks and basic render tests for the 16 missing pages, following the existing pattern of dynamic import + minimal prop scaffolding + `screen.getByRole` or `screen.getByText` assertion.
changes: resources/js/Pages/Admin/__smoke-tests__.test.tsx: Add tests for ProductAnalytics, NpsResponses, EmailSendLogs, ContactSubmissions (Index+Show), Roadmap (Index+Create), Sessions, Schedule, Cache, Users/Create, Feedback (Index+Show), Webhooks (Endpoints+IncomingWebhooks+DeliveryDetail)
verification: npm test -- --filter=smoke-tests passes with all new cases green

findings.title: NPS responses are read-only with no delete capability
findings.description: The NPS responses admin section has only `index` and `export` routes — there is no delete or bulk-delete. For a SaaS starter template this is a minor gap; product teams frequently need to purge test NPS responses or remove responses from internal team members. The pattern used by Contact Submissions (delete route with super_admin middleware) and Feedback (delete route with super_admin) would apply. This is lower priority because NPS data can be managed at DB level if needed, but it limits the template's operational completeness.

evidence.note: NPS routes: export and index only, no delete
Contact submissions has delete as reference pattern

implementation.approach: Add `destroy` method to AdminNpsResponsesController and a corresponding DELETE route with super_admin middleware. Add a confirm dialog in the UI for single-row delete. Optionally add bulk-delete following the feedback pattern.
changes: app/Http/Controllers/Admin/AdminNpsResponsesController.php: Add destroy() method with audit log
routes/admin.php: Add DELETE /nps-responses/{npsResponse} with throttle:10,1 and super_admin
resources/js/Pages/Admin/NpsResponses/Index.tsx: Add delete action with confirm dialog
verification: super_admin can delete an NPS response
Regular admin gets 403 on delete attempt
